title: Classical Ciphers and Cryptanalysis as Defect Labs · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go One Time Pad Conditions
summary: A training tool presents Caesar, substitution, repeating-key XOR, and one-time pads as equivalent encryption choices and accidentally ships the toy implementation. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the one time pad conditions increment in classical cipher attack and migration dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Explain one-time-pad key length, uniformity, secrecy, single use, distribution, and destruction requirements and their operational cost.
Demonstrate how repeated XOR keystream use reveals relationships between plaintexts without presenting XOR alone as production encryption.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go One Time Pad Conditions · Theory Crypto Go One Time Pad Condi…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 6042 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain one-time-pad key length, uniformity, secrecy, single use, distribution, and destruction requirements and their operational cost.
